# Fix: DockLayoutAdapter spec-chain unloadable in unit env (DockTabSortZone → DragCoordinator → Window import-time currentWorker.on)

## Context

Mnemo's #15017 (merged) added `DockLayoutAdapter → DockTabSortZone → DragCoordinator (module-scope) → Window`. `Window` is a `@singleton` that `Neo.setupClass` instantiates at import, and its `construct()` touches `Neo.currentWorker.on`. In the unit env, importing the adapter (for its own specs or **any** dashboard-classified spec) evaluates this chain **before** `setup()`'s `Neo.currentWorker` stub runs → crash → "No tests found" (the chain is unloadable). Masked at merge by test-scope classification.

**Blocks (why this is re-homed + urgent):** my #14771 RA-2 falsifier tests ([PR #15062](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/15062)) **and** Euclid's #14771 isolation testing — his cycle-1 review hit the same wall ("Local single-file adapter execution reaches the pre-existing `Neo.currentWorker.on` setup-order failure before discovery; it cannot validate this runtime plugin in isolation"). Re-homed from Mnemosyne (going dark 2026-07-13) — I'm absorbing it.

## Root cause (V-B-A'd on `dev`)

- `src/dashboard/DockTabSortZone.mjs:1` — `import DragCoordinator` (module-scope)
- `src/manager/DragCoordinator.mjs:3` — `import Window`
- `src/manager/Window.mjs:40-45` — `construct()` → `Neo.currentWorker.on({…})`; `:196` `Neo.setupClass(Window)` instantiates the singleton at import
- `test/playwright/setup.mjs:143` — the `Neo.currentWorker ??= {…}` stub is **inside** `setup()` (line 48) → ESM hoists the import chain before `setup()` runs

## Recommended fix — lazy the DragCoordinator dependency

`DockTabSortZone` uses `DragCoordinator` **only in runtime drag methods** (`onDragEnd:170`, `onDragMove:212`), never at module/class scope. Make the import lazy: preload in `construct`/`onConstructed` (`import('../manager/DragCoordinator.mjs').then(m => me.dragCoordinator = m.default)`) and use `me.dragCoordinator` at drag time. This breaks the module-scope chain (importing `DockTabSortZone` — which the adapter does for its config `module:` ref — no longer pulls `Window` at import → the adapter spec loads) while guaranteeing runtime availability (the preload resolves in ms; a drag is a much-later user gesture). The adapter spec imports `DockTabSortZone` but never **constructs** it, so the preload never fires in the unit env.

**Alternatives weighed:** (b) defer `Window.construct`'s `currentWorker.on` — bigger blast (touches the window-manager runtime); (c) hoist-safe test-infra stub — fragile, spec-import-order-dependent.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The `DockLayoutAdapter` spec chain LOADS in the unit env (no `currentWorker.on` crash / "No tests found"); the adapter spec + #14771 RA-2 falsifiers run.
- [ ] **Cross-window tab drag still works** (browser verification — the lazy preload resolves before any drag; assert via the #14772 recipe class). This is the safety AC — the fix touches drag-critical code.
- [ ] No module-scope import of `DragCoordinator`/`Window` reachable from the `DockLayoutAdapter` import chain (grep-guard).

## Related

`#15017` (the introducing PR) · `#14771` / PR `#15062` (blocked — my RA-2 tests) · `#14591` (whitebox-e2e) · `#14772` (cross-window drag recipe). Origin: routed to Mnemo via A2A during #14771 (no ticket then, per the routed-to-owner rule); minted now as the owner goes dark.

- 2026-07-12T03:47:06Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `a2a81d0` - "fix(dashboard): lazy the DockTabSortZone DragCoordinator import — adapter spec loadable in unit env (#15065)

The adapter statically imports DockTabSortZone for its projected sortZoneConfig, and
DockTabSortZone imported DragCoordinator at module scope → DragCoordinator imports Window,
a @singleton whose construct() touches Neo.currentWorker.on at setupClass/import. In the
unit loader (setup.mjs's currentWorker stub is inside setup(), hoisted after the imports)
that crashed the whole chain — DockLayoutAdapter.spec.mjs surfaced as 'No tests found',
also blocking #14771 RA-2 falsifiers (PR #15062) and Euclid's isolation testing.

DockTabSortZone uses DragCoordinator only in its async, sortGroup-gated drag methods
(processDragEnd, onDragMove), never module/class scope — resolve it via a cached-promise
lazy import at drag time. The adapter imports the SortZone but never constructs/drags it, so
the Window chain never evaluates in the unit env. processDragEnd's ordering (coordinator arms
remoteDropCommitted before the local check) is preserved.

Verified: DockLayoutAdapter.spec.mjs now LOADS — 19/19 pass (was unloadable). Residual: the
cross-window-drag runtime is designed-safe (lazy load resolves before any deliberate drag;
call semantics unchanged) but wants a browser cross-window-drag falsifier (post-merge)."
- 2026-07-12T03:50:12Z @neo-opus-grace cross-referenced by PR #15067
- 2026-07-12T04:36:54Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `354ea63` - "fix(dashboard): engage cross-window coordinator on move-entry + preload at construct (#15065)

Cycle-1 cross-family review (Euclid) found a first-gesture move/end ordering race the
cycle-1 lazy import introduced. Verified: Neo.manager.DomEvent.fire invokes drag handlers
WITHOUT awaiting them, and base container/SortZone.onDragMove awaits me.timeout(30) (a
one-frame macrotask) in its edge auto-scroll branch. So a fast release runs processDragEnd's
synchronous coordinator-end before a suspended onDragMove's coordinator-engage — a missed
remote engagement/drop, not the 'one-frame lag' the cycle-1 body claimed.

Fix (both halves off the drag hot path):
- construct() preloads DragCoordinator for a sortGroup zone into the synchronous dragCoordinator
  handle, so the drag handlers never resolve a module mid-gesture. Import stays out of module
  scope, so the adapter's static import of this class still never pulls the Window chain (the
  #15065 loader AC is preserved — DockLayoutAdapter.spec still loads 19/19).
- onDragMove engages the coordinator SYNCHRONOUSLY on entry, before super.onDragMove's awaits.
  Both coordinator methods are synchronous and screen-space, independent of the local sort, so
  engage-on-entry pairs with processDragEnd's synchronous coordinator-end: move-before-end holds
  regardless of the base suspension.

Regression: a fire-and-forget test invokes move+end WITHOUT awaiting move first and asserts
engage-before-end + transfer-once + suppress-once. Empirically falsified — it FAILS when the
engage sits after super (the pre-fix ordering), passes with the fix.

Verified: DockLayoutAdapter.spec 19/19 + DockCrossWindowParticipation.spec 9/9 = 28 passed.
Still open on #15065: the two-window browser AC (AC2), owner Grace."
- 2026-07-12T05:59:04Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `a6e3ba2` - "test(dashboard): cross-window drag safety witness — cold-first-gesture L3 receipt (#15065)

Cycle-2 review required #15065's two-window browser safety AC before the closing PR merges. The
dock product example is single-workspace and cannot produce it, and no automated two-window
journey exists (#14772's demo scene is still open + blocked on this fix), so this adds a
verification-only harness — NOT that demo.

examples/dashboard/crossWindowWitness: a minimal app whose construct runs the exact
DockCrossWindowParticipation cross-window composition (registered remote target + a real
DockTabSortZone source in the same sortGroup, the REAL DragCoordinator + executor) in a real
browser App-Worker, driving the FIRST gesture COLD — no resolveDragCoordinator await; the drop
depends entirely on construct()'s off-hot-path preload. It stamps the outcome onto the
witness-result component's vdom data-attributes.

DockCrossWindowWitnessNL.spec.mjs: loads the harness via the neuralLink fixture and asserts the
exact AC — cold first gesture -> transferItem commits once, source local drop suppressed once,
suppression flag consumed, coordinator warmed by construct-preload.

Green on the fix head: transferCount 1, dropFires 0, remoteDropCommitted false, coordinatorWarmed true."
- 2026-07-12T06:09:49Z @tobiu referenced in commit `0401d09` - "fix(dashboard): lazy the DockTabSortZone DragCoordinator import — adapter spec loadable (#15065) (#15067)

* fix(dashboard): lazy the DockTabSortZone DragCoordinator import — adapter spec loadable in unit env (#15065)

The adapter statically imports DockTabSortZone for its projected sortZoneConfig, and
DockTabSortZone imported DragCoordinator at module scope → DragCoordinator imports Window,
a @singleton whose construct() touches Neo.currentWorker.on at setupClass/import. In the
unit loader (setup.mjs's currentWorker stub is inside setup(), hoisted after the imports)
that crashed the whole chain — DockLayoutAdapter.spec.mjs surfaced as 'No tests found',
also blocking #14771 RA-2 falsifiers (PR #15062) and Euclid's isolation testing.

DockTabSortZone uses DragCoordinator only in its async, sortGroup-gated drag methods
(processDragEnd, onDragMove), never module/class scope — resolve it via a cached-promise
lazy import at drag time. The adapter imports the SortZone but never constructs/drags it, so
the Window chain never evaluates in the unit env. processDragEnd's ordering (coordinator arms
remoteDropCommitted before the local check) is preserved.

Verified: DockLayoutAdapter.spec.mjs now LOADS — 19/19 pass (was unloadable). Residual: the
cross-window-drag runtime is designed-safe (lazy load resolves before any deliberate drag;
call semantics unchanged) but wants a browser cross-window-drag falsifier (post-merge).

* fix(dashboard): engage cross-window coordinator on move-entry + preload at construct (#15065)

Cycle-1 cross-family review (Euclid) found a first-gesture move/end ordering race the
cycle-1 lazy import introduced. Verified: Neo.manager.DomEvent.fire invokes drag handlers
WITHOUT awaiting them, and base container/SortZone.onDragMove awaits me.timeout(30) (a
one-frame macrotask) in its edge auto-scroll branch. So a fast release runs processDragEnd's
synchronous coordinator-end before a suspended onDragMove's coordinator-engage — a missed
remote engagement/drop, not the 'one-frame lag' the cycle-1 body claimed.

Fix (both halves off the drag hot path):
- construct() preloads DragCoordinator for a sortGroup zone into the synchronous dragCoordinator
  handle, so the drag handlers never resolve a module mid-gesture. Import stays out of module
  scope, so the adapter's static import of this class still never pulls the Window chain (the
  #15065 loader AC is preserved — DockLayoutAdapter.spec still loads 19/19).
- onDragMove engages the coordinator SYNCHRONOUSLY on entry, before super.onDragMove's awaits.
  Both coordinator methods are synchronous and screen-space, independent of the local sort, so
  engage-on-entry pairs with processDragEnd's synchronous coordinator-end: move-before-end holds
  regardless of the base suspension.

Regression: a fire-and-forget test invokes move+end WITHOUT awaiting move first and asserts
engage-before-end + transfer-once + suppress-once. Empirically falsified — it FAILS when the
engage sits after super (the pre-fix ordering), passes with the fix.

Verified: DockLayoutAdapter.spec 19/19 + DockCrossWindowParticipation.spec 9/9 = 28 passed.
Still open on #15065: the two-window browser AC (AC2), owner Grace.

* test(dashboard): cross-window drag safety witness — cold-first-gesture L3 receipt (#15065)

Cycle-2 review required #15065's two-window browser safety AC before the closing PR merges. The
dock product example is single-workspace and cannot produce it, and no automated two-window
journey exists (#14772's demo scene is still open + blocked on this fix), so this adds a
verification-only harness — NOT that demo.

examples/dashboard/crossWindowWitness: a minimal app whose construct runs the exact
DockCrossWindowParticipation cross-window composition (registered remote target + a real
DockTabSortZone source in the same sortGroup, the REAL DragCoordinator + executor) in a real
browser App-Worker, driving the FIRST gesture COLD — no resolveDragCoordinator await; the drop
depends entirely on construct()'s off-hot-path preload. It stamps the outcome onto the
witness-result component's vdom data-attributes.

DockCrossWindowWitnessNL.spec.mjs: loads the harness via the neuralLink fixture and asserts the
exact AC — cold first gesture -> transferItem commits once, source local drop suppressed once,
suppression flag consumed, coordinator warmed by construct-preload.

Green on the fix head: transferCount 1, dropFires 0, remoteDropCommitted false, coordinatorWarmed true."
